questions.question: Compare the TCP/IP model with the OSI model. Give at least four points of difference.
keyPoints: TCP/IP has 4 layers, OSI has 7 layers
TCP/IP is a practical implementation, OSI is a reference model
TCP/IP combines Application, Presentation, Session layers into one
OSI provides clear separation of concerns
TCP/IP protocols are actual standards used on internet
OSI model was developed after TCP/IP existed

questions.question: Explain the DHCP process (DORA) with a neat diagram.
keyPoints: DHCP Discover - client broadcasts to find servers
DHCP Offer - server offers available IP address
DHCP Request - client requests the offered IP
DHCP Acknowledge - server acknowledges and finalizes lease
Process uses UDP ports 67 (server) and 68 (client)

questions.question: List and explain the functions of the four layers of TCP/IP protocol suite.
keyPoints: Application Layer: user interface, file transfer, email etc.
Transport Layer: end-to-end communication, reliability (TCP/UDP)
Internet Layer: logical addressing, routing (IP)
Network Access Layer: physical transmission, MAC addresses

questions.question: Explain the three-way handshake mechanism used by TCP to establish a connection.
keyPoints: Step 1: SYN - Client sends SYN segment with sequence number
Step 2: SYN-ACK - Server responds with SYN+ACK
Step 3: ACK - Client sends final ACK to confirm
This establishes a reliable connection before data transfer

questions.question: Differentiate between TCP and UDP protocols.
keyPoints: TCP is connection-oriented, UDP is connectionless
TCP provides reliability (acknowledgments, retransmission)
TCP has flow control and congestion control
UDP is faster but unreliable
TCP ensures ordered delivery, UDP does not
Applications: TCP for web, email; UDP for video streaming, gaming

questions.question: What is the purpose of DNS? Explain the types of DNS records.
keyPoints: DNS translates domain names to IP addresses
A record: maps domain to IPv4 address
AAAA record: maps domain to IPv6 address
MX record: mail exchange servers
CNAME: canonical name/alias
NS record: name servers

questions.question: Explain the concept of subnetting with an example.
keyPoints: Subnetting divides a network into smaller sub-networks
Subnet mask determines network and host portions
Example: 192.168.1.0/24 can be divided into /26 subnets
Each subnet has network address, host addresses, broadcast address
Subnetting improves security and reduces broadcast domain
